name: Tradicional
description: Baseline forfeits — flirtatious, not explicit. The core Picante experience.

forfeits.text: {{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly and {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Refuse and {{drawer}} takes it.
{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly and {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Refuse and {{drawer}} takes it.

{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} a truth (no yes/no questions). Answer honestly and {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Refuse and {{drawer}} takes it.
True or false? {{drawer}} makes a statement about themselves. Distribute 10 penalties among the wrong-guessers. If everyone guesses right, {{drawer}} takes them.
{{drawer}} reveals a spicy secret about themselves. If it is news to everyone, {{drawer}} distributes 10 penalties. If anyone already knew, {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} something darker — deeper, more intimate, more revealing. Answer → {{ccw}} takes 15. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 15.
Ace of Secretos. The three players with the highest penalty counts catch a blast: 15 / 10 / 5. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5 on top.
{{cw}} gives {{drawer}} a dare. Complete it → {{cw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.

{{cw}} gives {{drawer}} a dare. Complete it → {{cw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{drawer}} dares {{biasedRandom}} to do something. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Refuse → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{drawer}} is invited to send a risqué message to {{biasedRandom}}. Send it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Refuse → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{cw}} gives {{drawer}} a significantly harder dare. Complete it → {{cw}} takes 15. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 15.
Ace of Riesgos. The player with the lowest penalty count takes half the gap to the highest. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks a superlative. Group votes. {{drawer}} and the most-voted each take the penalty.

{{drawer}} and {{biasedRandom}} share the blame. Both take 10.
{{biasedRandom}} is invited to share a kiss with {{drawer}}. Both in → distribute 10 among everyone else. One declines → they take 10. Both decline → 5 each.
{{drawer}} and {{biasedRandom}} are locked together. Both take 15.
Ace of Sorbo. The three players with the lowest penalty counts catch up: 15 / 10 / 5. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
Mini-game: Index. Everyone touches their index finger to their nose. First to break loses 2.
Mini-game: Three-word. Starting with {{drawer}}, build a sentence one word at a time around the table. First to break the chain (or stall too long) loses 3.
Mini-game: Categories. {{drawer}} picks a category. Going clockwise, each player names an item. Repeat or blank and you lose 4.
Mini-game: Thumb War. {{drawer}} picks any challenger. Loser takes 5.
Mini-game: Countdown. {{drawer}} counts silently in their head. Everyone shouts 'STOP' when they think 10 seconds have passed. Furthest from 10 loses 6.
Mini-game: Rhyme Chain. {{drawer}} says a word. Going clockwise, each player must rhyme. Break the chain → lose 7.
Mini-game: Staring. {{drawer}} picks any challenger. First to blink, laugh, or look away loses 8.

Mini-game: Odds. {{drawer}} challenges any player: 'what are the odds you'd X?' They pick a ceiling. On 3-2-1 both say a number in range. Match → challenged does it or takes 9. No match → no one takes it.
Mini-game: Most-Likely. {{drawer}} poses a 'who's most likely to X?' question. Group votes. Most-voted takes 10.
Drawer's Challenge. {{drawer}} invents a short challenge (physical, trivia, or dexterity) and picks any player to attempt it. Complete → {{drawer}} takes 10. Fail → they take 10.
1v1 Rock-Paper-Scissors. {{drawer}} picks any player. One round. Loser takes 10.
Picante Roulette. On '3', every player (including {{drawer}}) simultaneously points at one other. Most-pointed-at takes 15. Tie → {{drawer}} chooses.
Ace of Locura. The player with the highest penalty count takes half the gap to the lowest. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
